codeReview.summary: Pass. The fix closes the original VAL-CMD-017 gap by stat-checking the resolved cwd before bridge lookup/spawn (`packages/webapp/src/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.ts:231-244`), returning a clean non-zero stderr result for nonexistent and non-directory paths. The new regression block in `packages/webapp/tests/shell/supplemental-commands/agent-command.test.ts:244-305` covers both failure branches plus the existing-directory happy path, and the updated full-stack helpers now delegate `stat`/`exists` to VirtualFS (`packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:177-190`, `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-bridge-hook.test.ts:144-157`) so command→hook coverage still exercises the new preflight behavior on real directories.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `webapp-worker` skill explains mock `CommandContext` usage and separately requires real ScoopContext/runtime-parity coverage, but it does not spell out that command-layer validations depending on `ctx.fs` need the shell context wired to a real/VirtualFS as well.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: This fix had to retrofit `createMockShellCtx()` in `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-integration.test.ts:177-190` and `packages/webapp/tests/scoops/agent-bridge-hook.test.ts:144-157` so `stat`/`exists` delegate to VirtualFS, plus update the affected call sites (`agent-integration.test.ts:405,443,473,496,522,613,668,708,747`; `agent-bridge-hook.test.ts:337,369`). By comparison, `.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:50` only calls out mock `CommandContext`, while the authenticity guidance at lines 77-80 discusses real ScoopContext and distinct runtime harnesses but not FS-backed shell contexts.

summary: Pass: commit 5b4bf21 fixes the prior blocking cwd-validation gap, keeps valid cwd behavior intact, and adds focused regression coverage with no blocking or non-blocking code issues found.
